title: GameStop Q4 and Fiscal 2025 Earnings Summary
text: GameStop's Q4 2025 net sales reached $1.104 billion, down from $1.283 billion in the prior year. The company reported net income of $127.9 million compared to $131.3 million the previous year. Operating efficiency improved significantly, with SG&A expenses declining to $241.5 million from $282.5 million year-over-year. Operating income surged to $135.2 million versus $79.8 million in Q4 2024. For fiscal year 2025, net sales totaled $3.630 billion, slightly below the prior year's $3.823 billion. However, profitability improved dramatically: net income reached $418.4 million compared to $131.3 million in fiscal 2024. The company transformed from an operating loss of $26.2 million in 2024 to operating income of $232.1 million in 2025. GameStop significantly strengthened its financial position, with cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities reaching 9.0 billion at year-end versus $4.8 billion previously. The company holds $368.4 million in Bitcoin and related receivables. Free cash flow for the full year was $597.3 million. Adjusted net income (non-GAAP) was $647.4 million. The company issued $4.2 billion in convertible notes during fiscal 2025, strengthening its cash position substantially.
